title: Porpax reticosa
intro: Porpax reticosa, commonly known as the nettle tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. The nettle tree has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering relief from inflammatory conditions, such as arthritis and gout, due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, as well as its ability to support digestive health. The bioactive constituents of Porpax reticosa include flavonoids, terpenoids, and glycosides, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with the nettle tree include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, pain, and inflammation.
taxonomy: Porpax reticosa is classified as a species of orchid within the family Orchidaceae. This classification places it within the order Asparagales and the family Orchidaceae. Further, it is placed within the subfamily Orchidoideae and tribe Cranichideae. Within the tribe, it is placed within the subtribe Orchidinae. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships.
